Overlapping_pathway_DESeq2

Author

Pakorn Sagulkoo

After we carried out psedobulk gene expression analysis and GSEA for several RT scRNA-sequences datasets, we will evaluate the consistency of functional enrichment results across those studiescalculated from Pseudobulk analysis using DESeq2. Then we will explore overlapping enrichment terms among those studies.

Import the relevant packages and set working path directory

# Import the relavant packages
library(UpSetR)
library(dplyr)
library(tibble)
library(stringr)
library(ggplot2)
library(ggrepel)
library(pheatmap)
library(viridis)
# Check root path
here::here()
[1] "/mnt/hp-storage/users/pakorns"

Define paths

# Define paths to load and save files
path_to_single_cell_analysis <- here::here("scRNAseq/systematic_review")

Load data and functions

# Source functions
source(here::here("bin/utils.R"))

Define names used in files and labels

# List file names of all studies
study.lst <- c("1-Penter_2021_GSE165087", "2-Nadeu_2022", "3-Hing_2023_GSE205704", "4-Parry_2023b", "5-Rigo_2024")
study.lst
[1] "1-Penter_2021_GSE165087" "2-Nadeu_2022"           
[3] "3-Hing_2023_GSE205704"   "4-Parry_2023b"          
[5] "5-Rigo_2024"            
# Create names for list later
names.lst <- sub(
  "^\\d+-([A-Za-z]+)_([0-9]{4}[A-Za-z]?).*$",  # capture name and year
  "\\1(\\2)",                         # output as Name(Year)
  study.lst
)
names.lst
[1] "Penter(2021)" "Nadeu(2022)"  "Hing(2023)"   "Parry(2023b)" "Rigo(2024)"  
# Extract only name of studies
study_names <- sub("^\\d+-([^_0-9]+).*", "\\1", study.lst)
study_names
[1] "Penter" "Nadeu"  "Hing"   "Parry"  "Rigo"

We performed GSEA using five main terms such as Hallmark, GO:BP, KEGG, Reactome, and WikiPath. We will identify these significant terms in both up- and downregulation.

Overlapping of GSEA results based on DESeq2-based pseudobulk analysis

Hallmark

# Import the results from all studies and collect in a list
path_to_download <- str_c(path_to_single_cell_analysis, "/", study.lst, "/pseudo_bulk_deseq2/", study_names, "_deseq2_hm_gsea.rds")
path_to_download <- path_to_download
hm_gsea.lst <- purrr::pmap(
  list(path_to_download), 
  function(path){
    gsea <- readRDS(path)
    return(gsea)
  }
)
names(hm_gsea.lst) <- names.lst

Upregulated terms

# Extract adjusted significant upregulated hallmark terms
sig_term.lst <- purrr::pmap(
  list(hm_gsea.lst),
  function(gsea){
    selected_term <- gsea@result[gsea@result$NES > 0, "ID"]
    return(selected_term)
  }
)

# Reorder the list first
sig_term.lst <- sig_term.lst %>%
  purrr::discard(~ length(.x) == 0) %>%   # drop empty ones
  rev()                                   # reverse order

# Convert to UpSetR input
upset_data <- fromList(sig_term.lst)

# Plot UpSetR
upset.plt <- upset(
  upset_data,
  sets = colnames(upset_data),
  keep.order = TRUE,
  nintersects = NA,
  point.size = 3.5,
  line.size = 1,
  text.scale = c(2, 2, 2, 2, 2.5, 2.5), # defined as c(intersection size title, intersection size tick labels, set size title, set size tick labels, set names, numbers above bars)
  mb.ratio = c(0.55, 0.45) # Adjust margin: give more space to the bottom/left for those large labels
)

upset.plt
# Save the figure
png(filename = paste0(path_to_save_figure, "/upset_plt_deseq2_up_gsea.png"), width=12, height=8, units="in", res=500)
print(upset.plt)
dev.off()

In comparison with the results from Seurat-based pseudobulk approach, DESeq2-based result has a higher common terms than the previous approach. Then we will identify those common terms. This will be explored using set operations and UpSet plots. For example, if we have three sets (A, B, and C), the expression ‘members from A ∩ B’ refers to terms that are present in both A and B but excluded from those present in all three sets (A, B, and C).

# Find common hallmark terms in all studies
five_common_hm <- Reduce(intersect, sig_term.lst)
five_common_hm
[1] "HALLMARK_OXIDATIVE_PHOSPHORYLATION" "HALLMARK_FATTY_ACID_METABOLISM"    
[3] "HALLMARK_G2M_CHECKPOINT"            "HALLMARK_MTORC1_SIGNALING"         
[5] "HALLMARK_E2F_TARGETS"               "HALLMARK_MYC_TARGETS_V1"           
# Find common hallmark terms in four studies: Nadeu, Hing, Parry, and Rigo
four_common_hm <- Reduce(intersect, sig_term.lst[names(sig_term.lst) != "Penter(2021)"])
setdiff(four_common_hm, five_common_hm)
[1] "HALLMARK_CHOLESTEROL_HOMEOSTASIS"
# Find common hallmark terms in four studies: Penter, Nadeu, Hing, and Rigo
four_common_hm <- Reduce(intersect, sig_term.lst[names(sig_term.lst) != "Parry(2023b)"])
setdiff(four_common_hm, five_common_hm)
[1] "HALLMARK_ADIPOGENESIS" "HALLMARK_DNA_REPAIR"  
# Find common hallmark terms in four studies: Penter, Nadeu, Hing, and Parry
four_common_hm <- Reduce(intersect, sig_term.lst[names(sig_term.lst) != "Rigo(2024)"])
setdiff(four_common_hm, five_common_hm)
[1] "HALLMARK_MYC_TARGETS_V2" "HALLMARK_GLYCOLYSIS"

These common upregulated hallmark terms were related to OXOSPHOS, glucose and lipid metabolisms, E2F and Myc signalling, and Cell cycle and proliferation.

Downregulated terms

We will exclude Rigo(2024) from the overapping downregulated hallmark terms as no significant downregulated terms were detected.

# Extract adjusted significant downregulated hallmark terms
sig_term.lst <- purrr::pmap(
  list(hm_gsea.lst),
  function(gsea){
    selected_term <- gsea@result[gsea@result$NES < 0, "ID"]
    return(selected_term)
  }
)

# Reorder the list first
sig_term.lst <- sig_term.lst %>%
  purrr::discard(~ length(.x) == 0) %>%   # drop empty ones
  rev()                                   # reverse order

# Convert to UpSetR input
upset_data <- fromList(sig_term.lst)

# Plot UpSetR
upset.plt <- upset(
  upset_data,
  sets = colnames(upset_data),
  keep.order = TRUE,
  nintersects = NA,
  point.size = 3.5,
  line.size = 1,
  text.scale = c(2, 2, 2, 2, 2.5, 2.5), # defined as c(intersection size title, intersection size tick labels, set size title, set size tick labels, set names, numbers above bars)
  mb.ratio = c(0.55, 0.45) # Adjust margin: give more space to the bottom/left for those large labels
)

upset.plt
# Save the figure
png(filename = paste0(path_to_save_figure, "/upset_plt_deseq2_down_gsea.png"), width=12, height=8, units="in", res=500)
print(upset.plt)
dev.off()

Although no common terms were found in all four studies, we will idenity common terms in two and three studies.

# Find common hallmark terms in three studies: Penter, Nadeu, and Hing
three_common_hm <- Reduce(intersect, sig_term.lst[names(sig_term.lst) != "Parry(2023b)"])
three_common_hm
[1] "HALLMARK_TNFA_SIGNALING_VIA_NFKB"
# Find common hallmark terms in three studies: Nadeu and Hing
two_common_hm <- Reduce(intersect, sig_term.lst[c("Nadeu(2022)", "Hing(2023)")])
setdiff(two_common_hm, three_common_hm)
[1] "HALLMARK_TGF_BETA_SIGNALING"
# Find common hallmark terms in three studies: Penter and Nadeu
two_common_hm <- Reduce(intersect, sig_term.lst[c("Penter(2021)", "Nadeu(2022)")])
setdiff(two_common_hm, three_common_hm)
[1] "HALLMARK_EPITHELIAL_MESENCHYMAL_TRANSITION"
[2] "HALLMARK_INFLAMMATORY_RESPONSE"

GO:BP

# Import the results from all studies and collect in a list
path_to_download <- str_c(path_to_single_cell_analysis, "/", study.lst, "/pseudo_bulk_deseq2/", study_names, "_deseq2_go_gsea.rds")
path_to_download <- path_to_download
go_gsea.lst <- purrr::pmap(
  list(path_to_download), 
  function(path){
    gsea <- readRDS(path)
    return(gsea)
  }
)
names(go_gsea.lst) <- names.lst

Upregulated terms

# Extract adjusted significant upregulated GO:BP terms
sig_term.lst <- purrr::pmap(
  list(go_gsea.lst),
  function(gsea){
    selected_term <- gsea@result[gsea@result$NES > 0, "ID"]
    return(selected_term)
  }
)

# Reorder the list first
sig_term.lst <- sig_term.lst %>%
  purrr::discard(~ length(.x) == 0) %>%   # drop empty ones
  rev()                                   # reverse order

# Convert to UpSetR input
upset_data <- fromList(sig_term.lst)

# Plot UpSetR
upset.plt <- upset(
  upset_data,
  sets = colnames(upset_data),
  keep.order = TRUE,
  nintersects = NA,
  point.size = 3.5,
  line.size = 1,
  text.scale = c(2, 2, 2, 2, 2.5, 2.5), # defined as c(intersection size title, intersection size tick labels, set size title, set size tick labels, set names, numbers above bars)
  mb.ratio = c(0.55, 0.45) # Adjust margin: give more space to the bottom/left for those large labels
)

upset.plt
# Find common GO:BP terms in all studies
five_common_go <- Reduce(intersect, sig_term.lst)
five_common_go
[1] "GOBP_OXIDATIVE_PHOSPHORYLATION"                   
[2] "GOBP_NUCLEOSIDE_PHOSPHATE_BIOSYNTHETIC_PROCESS"   
[3] "GOBP_NUCLEOSIDE_TRIPHOSPHATE_METABOLIC_PROCESS"   
[4] "GOBP_RIBOSE_PHOSPHATE_BIOSYNTHETIC_PROCESS"       
[5] "GOBP_NUCLEOSIDE_TRIPHOSPHATE_BIOSYNTHETIC_PROCESS"
[6] "GOBP_SMALL_MOLECULE_CATABOLIC_PROCESS"            
[7] "GOBP_MITOCHONDRIAL_TRANSPORT"                     
[8] "GOBP_CHROMOSOME_SEGREGATION"                      
# Find common GO:BP terms in four studies: Penter, Nadeu, Parry, and Rigo
four_common_go <- Reduce(intersect, sig_term.lst[names(sig_term.lst) != "Hing(2023)"])
setdiff(four_common_go, five_common_go)
[1] "GOBP_ORGANIC_ACID_CATABOLIC_PROCESS"
# Find common GO:BP terms in four studies: Nadeu, Hing, Parry, and Rigo
four_common_go <- Reduce(intersect, sig_term.lst[names(sig_term.lst) != "Penter(2021)"])
setdiff(four_common_go, five_common_go)
[1] "GOBP_MITOCHONDRION_ORGANIZATION"                  
[2] "GOBP_PURINE_CONTAINING_COMPOUND_METABOLIC_PROCESS"
[3] "GOBP_RIBOSE_PHOSPHATE_METABOLIC_PROCESS"          
[4] "GOBP_SMALL_MOLECULE_BIOSYNTHETIC_PROCESS"         
# Find common GO:BP terms in four studies: Penter, Nadeu, Hing, and Rigo
four_common_go <- Reduce(intersect, sig_term.lst[names(sig_term.lst) != "Parry(2023b)"])
setdiff(four_common_go, five_common_go)
 [1] "GOBP_AEROBIC_RESPIRATION"                                
 [2] "GOBP_CELLULAR_RESPIRATION"                               
 [3] "GOBP_GENERATION_OF_PRECURSOR_METABOLITES_AND_ENERGY"     
 [4] "GOBP_ATP_SYNTHESIS_COUPLED_ELECTRON_TRANSPORT"           
 [5] "GOBP_ENERGY_DERIVATION_BY_OXIDATION_OF_ORGANIC_COMPOUNDS"
 [6] "GOBP_ELECTRON_TRANSPORT_CHAIN"                           
 [7] "GOBP_MITOCHONDRIAL_TRANSLATION"                          
 [8] "GOBP_ATP_METABOLIC_PROCESS"                              
 [9] "GOBP_MITOCHONDRIAL_GENE_EXPRESSION"                      
[10] "GOBP_MITOCHONDRIAL_RESPIRATORY_CHAIN_COMPLEX_ASSEMBLY"   
[11] "GOBP_NADH_DEHYDROGENASE_COMPLEX_ASSEMBLY"                
[12] "GOBP_MITOCHONDRIAL_MEMBRANE_ORGANIZATION"                
[13] "GOBP_CYTOCHROME_COMPLEX_ASSEMBLY"                        
# Find common GO:BP terms in four studies: Penter, Nadeu, Hing, and Parry
four_common_go <- Reduce(intersect, sig_term.lst[names(sig_term.lst) != "Rigo(2024)"])
setdiff(four_common_go, five_common_go)
[1] "GOBP_REGULATION_OF_MITOTIC_NUCLEAR_DIVISION"
[2] "GOBP_NUCLEAR_CHROMOSOME_SEGREGATION"        
[3] "GOBP_MITOTIC_NUCLEAR_DIVISION"              
[4] "GOBP_MITOTIC_SISTER_CHROMATID_SEGREGATION"

The common upregulated GO:BP terms were Consistent with the common upregulated hallmark terms that the both terms were associated with OXPHOS and nucleotide metabolism. indicating increased cell proliferation activity.

Downregulated terms

# Extract adjusted significant downregulated GO:BP terms
sig_term.lst <- purrr::pmap(
  list(go_gsea.lst),
  function(gsea){
    selected_term <- gsea@result[gsea@result$NES < 0, "ID"]
    return(selected_term)
  }
)

# Reorder the list first
sig_term.lst <- sig_term.lst %>%
  purrr::discard(~ length(.x) == 0) %>%   # drop empty ones
  rev()                                   # reverse order

# Convert to UpSetR input
upset_data <- fromList(sig_term.lst)

# Plot UpSetR
upset.plt <- upset(
  upset_data,
  sets = colnames(upset_data),
  keep.order = TRUE,
  nintersects = NA,
  point.size = 3.5,
  line.size = 1,
  text.scale = c(2, 2, 2, 2, 2.5, 2.5), # defined as c(intersection size title, intersection size tick labels, set size title, set size tick labels, set names, numbers above bars)
  mb.ratio = c(0.55, 0.45) # Adjust margin: give more space to the bottom/left for those large labels
)

upset.plt

Overall, no common downregulated GO:BP terms in all studies were detected. We will idenity common terms in two studies.

# Common terms between Penter and Rigo
Reduce(intersect, sig_term.lst[c("Penter(2021)", "Rigo(2024)")])
[1] "GOBP_CONNECTIVE_TISSUE_DEVELOPMENT"
# Common terms between Penter and Parry
Reduce(intersect, sig_term.lst[c("Penter(2021)", "Parry(2023b)")])
[1] "GOBP_HUMORAL_IMMUNE_RESPONSE_MEDIATED_BY_CIRCULATING_IMMUNOGLOBULIN"

Reduce(intersect, sig_term.lst[c("Nadeu(2022)", "Hing(2023)")])
[1] "GOBP_POSITIVE_REGULATION_OF_MRNA_CATABOLIC_PROCESS"

Reduce(intersect, sig_term.lst[c("Nadeu(2022)", "Rigo(2024)")])

Reduce(intersect, sig_term.lst[c("Penter(2021)", "Nadeu(2022)")])

KEGG pathways

# Import the results from all studies and collect in a list
path_to_download <- str_c(path_to_single_cell_analysis, "/", study.lst, "/pseudo_bulk_deseq2/", study_names, "_deseq2_kegg_gsea.rds")
path_to_download <- path_to_download
kegg_gsea.lst <- purrr::pmap(
  list(path_to_download), 
  function(path){
    gsea <- readRDS(path)
    return(gsea)
  }
)
names(kegg_gsea.lst) <- names.lst

Upregulated terms

# Extract adjusted significant upregulated KEGG terms
sig_term.lst <- purrr::pmap(
  list(kegg_gsea.lst),
  function(gsea){
    selected_term <- gsea@result[gsea@result$NES > 0, "ID"]
    return(selected_term)
  }
)

# Reorder the list first
sig_term.lst <- sig_term.lst %>%
  purrr::discard(~ length(.x) == 0) %>%   # drop empty ones
  rev()                                   # reverse order

# Convert to UpSetR input
upset_data <- fromList(sig_term.lst)

# Plot UpSetR
upset.plt <- upset(
  upset_data,
  sets = colnames(upset_data),
  keep.order = TRUE,
  nintersects = NA,
  point.size = 3.5,
  line.size = 1,
  text.scale = c(2, 2, 2, 2, 2.5, 2.5), # defined as c(intersection size title, intersection size tick labels, set size title, set size tick labels, set names, numbers above bars)
  mb.ratio = c(0.55, 0.45) # Adjust margin: give more space to the bottom/left for those large labels
)

upset.plt

Although no common terms in all five studies were detected, we will identify common terms in four studies.

# Common KEGG terms in four studies: Penter, Nadeu, Hing, and Parry
Reduce(intersect, sig_term.lst[c("Penter(2021)", "Nadeu(2022)", "Hing(2023)", "Parry(2023b)")])
[1] "KEGG_PYRIMIDINE_METABOLISM"
# Common KEGG terms in four studies: Penter, Nadeu, Hing, and Rigo
Reduce(intersect, sig_term.lst[c("Penter(2021)", "Nadeu(2022)", "Hing(2023)", "Rigo(2024)")])
[1] "KEGG_OXIDATIVE_PHOSPHORYLATION" "KEGG_HUNTINGTONS_DISEASE"      
[3] "KEGG_PURINE_METABOLISM"

Generally, the common upregulated KEGG terms were related to OXPHOS and nucleotide metabolisms, consistent with hallmark and GO:BP upregulated terms.

Downregulated terms

As no significant downregulated KEGG terms in Rigo(2024) are detected, we will not show it.

# Extract adjusted significant downregulated KEGG terms
sig_term.lst <- purrr::pmap(
  list(kegg_gsea.lst),
  function(gsea){
    selected_term <- gsea@result[gsea@result$NES < 0, "ID"]
    return(selected_term)
  }
)

# Reorder the list first
sig_term.lst <- sig_term.lst %>%
  purrr::discard(~ length(.x) == 0) %>%   # drop empty ones
  rev()                                   # reverse order

# Convert to UpSetR input
upset_data <- fromList(sig_term.lst)

# Plot UpSetR
upset.plt <- upset(
  upset_data,
  sets = colnames(upset_data),
  keep.order = TRUE,
  nintersects = NA,
  point.size = 3.5,
  line.size = 1,
  text.scale = c(2, 2, 2, 2, 2.5, 2.5), # defined as c(intersection size title, intersection size tick labels, set size title, set size tick labels, set names, numbers above bars)
  mb.ratio = c(0.55, 0.45) # Adjust margin: give more space to the bottom/left for those large labels
)

upset.plt

No common terms are detected. We will identify common terms in two studies.

# Common terms between Penter and Parry
Reduce(intersect, sig_term.lst[c("Penter(2021)", "Parry(2023b)")])
[1] "KEGG_TOLL_LIKE_RECEPTOR_SIGNALING_PATHWAY"
# Common terms between Penter and Nadeu
Reduce(intersect, sig_term.lst[c("Penter(2021)", "Nadeu(2022)")])
[1] "KEGG_REGULATION_OF_ACTIN_CYTOSKELETON"
[2] "KEGG_MAPK_SIGNALING_PATHWAY"          
[3] "KEGG_NEUROTROPHIN_SIGNALING_PATHWAY"

Reactome pathways

# Import the results from all studies and collect in a list
path_to_download <- str_c(path_to_single_cell_analysis, "/", study.lst, "/pseudo_bulk_deseq2/", study_names, "_deseq2_reactome_gsea.rds")
path_to_download <- path_to_download
reactome_gsea.lst <- purrr::pmap(
  list(path_to_download), 
  function(path){
    gsea <- readRDS(path)
    return(gsea)
  }
)
names(reactome_gsea.lst) <- names.lst

Upregulated terms

# Extract adjusted significant upregulated Reactome terms
sig_term.lst <- purrr::pmap(
  list(reactome_gsea.lst),
  function(gsea){
    selected_term <- gsea@result[gsea@result$NES > 0, "ID"]
    return(selected_term)
  }
)

# Reorder the list first
sig_term.lst <- sig_term.lst %>%
  purrr::discard(~ length(.x) == 0) %>%   # drop empty ones
  rev()                                   # reverse order

# Convert to UpSetR input
upset_data <- fromList(sig_term.lst)

# Plot UpSetR
upset.plt <- upset(
  upset_data,
  sets = colnames(upset_data),
  keep.order = TRUE,
  nintersects = NA,
  point.size = 3.5,
  line.size = 1,
  text.scale = c(2, 2, 2, 2, 2.5, 2.5), # defined as c(intersection size title, intersection size tick labels, set size title, set size tick labels, set names, numbers above bars)
  mb.ratio = c(0.55, 0.45) # Adjust margin: give more space to the bottom/left for those large labels
)

upset.plt
# Common upregulated reactome terms in all studies
five_common_reactome <- Reduce(intersect, sig_term.lst)
five_common_reactome
[1] "REACTOME_MITOCHONDRIAL_TRANSLATION"   
[2] "REACTOME_MITOCHONDRIAL_PROTEIN_IMPORT"
[3] "REACTOME_CELL_CYCLE_CHECKPOINTS"      
[4] "REACTOME_METABOLISM_OF_NUCLEOTIDES"   
# Common upregulated reactome terms in four studies
four_common_reactome <- Reduce(intersect, sig_term.lst[names(sig_term.lst) != "Parry(2023b)"])
setdiff(four_common_reactome, five_common_reactome)
 [1] "REACTOME_AEROBIC_RESPIRATION_AND_RESPIRATORY_ELECTRON_TRANSPORT"                                                  
 [2] "REACTOME_RESPIRATORY_ELECTRON_TRANSPORT"                                                                          
 [3] "REACTOME_PROTEIN_LOCALIZATION"                                                                                    
 [4] "REACTOME_TRANSLATION"                                                                                             
 [5] "REACTOME_APC_C_MEDIATED_DEGRADATION_OF_CELL_CYCLE_PROTEINS"                                                       
 [6] "REACTOME_MITOTIC_G1_PHASE_AND_G1_S_TRANSITION"                                                                    
 [7] "REACTOME_APC_C_CDH1_MEDIATED_DEGRADATION_OF_CDC20_AND_OTHER_APC_C_CDH1_TARGETED_PROTEINS_IN_LATE_MITOSIS_EARLY_G1"
 [8] "REACTOME_ORC1_REMOVAL_FROM_CHROMATIN"                                                                             
 [9] "REACTOME_SWITCHING_OF_ORIGINS_TO_A_POST_REPLICATIVE_STATE"                                                        
[10] "REACTOME_DNA_REPLICATION_PRE_INITIATION"                                                                          
[11] "REACTOME_MITOCHONDRIAL_PROTEIN_DEGRADATION"                                                                       
[12] "REACTOME_DNA_REPLICATION"                                                                                         
[13] "REACTOME_COMPLEX_I_BIOGENESIS"                                                                                    
[14] "REACTOME_METABOLISM_OF_AMINO_ACIDS_AND_DERIVATIVES"                                                               
[15] "REACTOME_SYNTHESIS_OF_DNA"                                                                                        
[16] "REACTOME_S_PHASE"                                                                                                 
[17] "REACTOME_G2_M_CHECKPOINTS"                                                                                        
[18] "REACTOME_MITOTIC_METAPHASE_AND_ANAPHASE"                                                                          
[19] "REACTOME_SEPARATION_OF_SISTER_CHROMATIDS"                                                                         
[20] "REACTOME_METABOLISM_OF_VITAMINS_AND_COFACTORS"

Overall, the upregulated reactome terms were consistent with previous upregulated terms which were related to OXPHOS, nucleotide metabolisms, cell cycle, and cell proliferation.

Downregulated terms

# Extract adjusted significant downregulated Reactome terms
sig_term.lst <- purrr::pmap(
  list(reactome_gsea.lst),
  function(gsea){
    selected_term <- gsea@result[gsea@result$NES < 0, "ID"]
    return(selected_term)
  }
)

# Reorder the list first
sig_term.lst <- sig_term.lst %>%
  purrr::discard(~ length(.x) == 0) %>%   # drop empty ones
  rev()                                   # reverse order

# Convert to UpSetR input
upset_data <- fromList(sig_term.lst)

# Plot UpSetR
upset.plt <- upset(
  upset_data,
  sets = colnames(upset_data),
  keep.order = TRUE,
  nintersects = NA,
  point.size = 3.5,
  line.size = 1,
  text.scale = c(2, 2, 2, 2, 2.5, 2.5), # defined as c(intersection size title, intersection size tick labels, set size title, set size tick labels, set names, numbers above bars)
  mb.ratio = c(0.55, 0.45) # Adjust margin: give more space to the bottom/left for those large labels
)

upset.plt

No common terms are detected. We will identify common terms in two studies.

# Common terms between Penter and Parry
Reduce(intersect, sig_term.lst[c("Penter(2021)", "Parry(2023b)")])
[1] "REACTOME_TOLL_LIKE_RECEPTOR_CASCADES"
# Common terms between Nadeu and Parry
Reduce(intersect, sig_term.lst[c("Nadeu(2022)", "Parry(2023b)")])
[1] "REACTOME_PROCESSING_OF_CAPPED_INTRON_CONTAINING_PRE_MRNA"                    
[2] "REACTOME_SARS_COV_2_ACTIVATES_MODULATES_INNATE_AND_ADAPTIVE_IMMUNE_RESPONSES"
# Common terms between Penter and Nadeu
Reduce(intersect, sig_term.lst[c("Penter(2021)", "Nadeu(2022)")])
[1] "REACTOME_SIGNALING_BY_TGFB_FAMILY_MEMBERS"                             
[2] "REACTOME_TRANSCRIPTIONAL_REGULATION_OF_WHITE_ADIPOCYTE_DIFFERENTIATION"

WikiPath

# Import the results from all studies and collect in a list
path_to_download <- str_c(path_to_single_cell_analysis, "/", study.lst, "/pseudo_bulk_deseq2/", study_names, "_deseq2_wikipath_gsea.rds")
path_to_download <- path_to_download
wikipath_gsea.lst <- purrr::pmap(
  list(path_to_download), 
  function(path){
    gsea <- readRDS(path)
    return(gsea)
  }
)
names(wikipath_gsea.lst) <- names.lst

Upregulated terms

# Extract adjusted significant upregulated WikiPath terms
sig_term.lst <- purrr::pmap(
  list(wikipath_gsea.lst),
  function(gsea){
    selected_term <- gsea@result[gsea@result$NES > 0, "ID"]
    return(selected_term)
  }
)

# Reorder the list first
sig_term.lst <- sig_term.lst %>%
  purrr::discard(~ length(.x) == 0) %>%   # drop empty ones
  rev()                                   # reverse order

# Convert to UpSetR input
upset_data <- fromList(sig_term.lst)

# Plot UpSetR
upset.plt <- upset(
  upset_data,
  sets = colnames(upset_data),
  keep.order = TRUE,
  nintersects = NA,
  point.size = 3.5,
  line.size = 1,
  text.scale = c(2, 2, 2, 2, 2.5, 2.5), # defined as c(intersection size title, intersection size tick labels, set size title, set size tick labels, set names, numbers above bars)
  mb.ratio = c(0.55, 0.45) # Adjust margin: give more space to the bottom/left for those large labels
)

upset.plt

Although no common terms in all five studies were detected, we will identify common terms in four and three studies.

# Common WikiPath terms in four studies: Penter, Nadeu, Hing, and Rigo
four_common_wikipath <- Reduce(intersect, sig_term.lst[names(sig_term.lst) != "Parry(2023b)"])
four_common_wikipath 
[1] "WP_METABOLIC_REPROGRAMMING_IN_COLON_CANCER"             
[2] "WP_MITOCHONDRIAL_COMPLEX_I_ASSEMBLY_MODEL_OXPHOS_SYSTEM"
[3] "WP_GLYCOLYSIS_AND_GLUCONEOGENESIS"                      
# Common WikiPath terms in three studies: Hing, Parry, and Rigo
three_common_wikipath <- Reduce(intersect, sig_term.lst[names(sig_term.lst) %in% c("Penter(2021)", "Nadeu(2022)")])
setdiff(three_common_wikipath, four_common_wikipath)
[1] "WP_PYRIMIDINE_METABOLISM"                       
[2] "WP_AMINO_ACID_METABOLISM"                       
[3] "WP_DNA_REPAIR_PATHWAYS_FULL_NETWORK"            
[4] "WP_DNA_IRDAMAGE_AND_CELLULAR_RESPONSE_VIA_ATR"  
[5] "WP_METABOLIC_REPROGRAMMING_IN_PANCREATIC_CANCER"
# Common WikiPath terms in three studies: Nadeu, Hing, and Rigo
three_common_wikipath <- Reduce(intersect, sig_term.lst[!(names(sig_term.lst) %in% c("Penter(2021)", "Parry(2023b)"))])
setdiff(three_common_wikipath, four_common_wikipath)
[1] "WP_METABOLIC_EPILEPTIC_DISORDERS"
# Common WikiPath terms in three studies: Nadeu, Hing, and Parry
three_common_wikipath <- Reduce(intersect, sig_term.lst[!(names(sig_term.lst) %in% c("Penter(2021)", "Rigo(2024)"))])
setdiff(three_common_wikipath, four_common_wikipath)
[1] "WP_RETINOBLASTOMA_GENE_IN_CANCER"
# Common WikiPath terms in three studies: Penter, Nadeu, and Hing
three_common_wikipath <- Reduce(intersect, sig_term.lst[!(names(sig_term.lst) %in% c("Parry(2023b)", "Rigo(2024)"))])
setdiff(three_common_wikipath, four_common_wikipath)
[1] "WP_PYRIMIDINE_METABOLISM"                     
[2] "WP_DNA_IRDAMAGE_AND_CELLULAR_RESPONSE_VIA_ATR"
# Common WikiPath terms in three studies: Penter, Hing, and Rigo
three_common_wikipath <- Reduce(intersect, sig_term.lst[!(names(sig_term.lst) %in% c("Nadeu(2022)", "Parry(2023b)"))])
setdiff(three_common_wikipath, four_common_wikipath)
[1] "WP_ELECTRON_TRANSPORT_CHAIN_OXPHOS_SYSTEM_IN_MITOCHONDRIA"
[2] "WP_NONALCOHOLIC_FATTY_LIVER_DISEASE"                      
[3] "WP_PROTEASOME_DEGRADATION"                                
[4] "WP_OXIDATIVE_PHOSPHORYLATION"                             
[5] "WP_MITOCHONDRIAL_COMPLEX_IV_ASSEMBLY"

Breifly, the upregulated WikiPath terms were consistent with previous upregulated terms which were related to OXPHOS, nucleotide metabolisms, cell cycle, and cell proliferation.

Downregulated terms

# Extract adjusted significant downregulated WikiPath terms
sig_term.lst <- purrr::pmap(
  list(wikipath_gsea.lst),
  function(gsea){
    selected_term <- gsea@result[gsea@result$NES < 0, "ID"]
    return(selected_term)
  }
)

# Reorder the list first
sig_term.lst <- sig_term.lst %>%
  purrr::discard(~ length(.x) == 0) %>%   # drop empty ones
  rev()                                   # reverse order

# Convert to UpSetR input
upset_data <- fromList(sig_term.lst)

# Plot UpSetR
upset.plt <- upset(
  upset_data,
  sets = colnames(upset_data),
  keep.order = TRUE,
  nintersects = NA,
  point.size = 3.5,
  line.size = 1,

  mb.ratio = c(0.55, 0.45) # Adjust margin: give more space to the bottom/left for those large labels

No common terms are detected. We will identify common terms in two studies.

# Common terms between Penter and Nadeu
Reduce(intersect, sig_term.lst[c("Penter(2021)", "Nadeu(2022)")])
[1] "WP_MAPK_SIGNALING"                                                 
[2] "WP_OSTEOARTHRITIC_CHONDROCYTE_HYPERTROPHY"                         
[3] "WP_CHROMOSOMAL_AND_MICROSATELLITE_INSTABILITY_IN_COLORECTAL_CANCER"
[4] "WP_INTERLEUKIN1_IL1_STRUCTURAL_PATHWAY"

Create common table result for common upregulated terms for Hallmark, GO:BP, and Reactome

# Hallmark
common_upreg_hm <- purrr::map(
  hm_gsea.lst,
  ~ .x@result %>%
    dplyr::filter(NES > 0) %>%
    pull(ID)
) %>%
  Reduce(intersect, .)

common_hm_nes.df <- purrr::map_dfr(
  names(hm_gsea.lst),
  function(study_name){
    gsea <- hm_gsea.lst[[study_name]]@result
    gsea_nes <- gsea %>%
      dplyr::filter(ID %in% common_upreg_hm) %>%
      dplyr::select(ID, NES) %>%
      dplyr::mutate(study = study_name) 
    return(gsea_nes)
  }
)

# Change the data frame to wider form
common_hm_nes.df <- common_hm_nes.df  %>%
  tidyr::pivot_wider(
    names_from = study,
    values_from = NES
  )

head(common_hm_nes.df)
# A tibble: 6 × 6
  ID       `Penter(2021)` `Nadeu(2022)` `Hing(2023)` `Parry(2023b)` `Rigo(2024)`
  <chr>             <dbl>         <dbl>        <dbl>          <dbl>        <dbl>
1 HALLMAR…           3.15          2.11         2.04           1.36         2.95
2 HALLMAR…           3.01          2.06         2.06           2.09         1.44
3 HALLMAR…           2.24          2.24         2.11           2.01         1.61
4 HALLMAR…           2.22          1.89         1.89           1.82         1.68
5 HALLMAR…           1.77          1.42         1.77           2.07         1.84
6 HALLMAR…           1.86          2.15         1.65           1.70         2.39
# GO:BP
common_upreg_go <- purrr::map(
  go_gsea.lst,
  ~ .x@result %>%
    dplyr::filter(NES > 0) %>%
    pull(ID)
) %>%
  Reduce(intersect, .)

common_go_nes.df <- purrr::map_dfr(
  names(go_gsea.lst),
  function(study_name){
    gsea <- go_gsea.lst[[study_name]]@result
    gsea_nes <- gsea %>%
      dplyr::filter(ID %in% common_upreg_go) %>%
      dplyr::select(ID, NES) %>%
      dplyr::mutate(study = study_name) 
    return(gsea_nes)
  }
)

# Change the data frame to wider form
common_go_nes.df <- common_go_nes.df  %>%
  tidyr::pivot_wider(
    names_from = study,
    values_from = NES
  )

head(common_go_nes.df)

  ID       `Penter(2021)` `Nadeu(2022)` `Hing(2023)` `Parry(2023b)` `Rigo(2024)`
  <chr>             <dbl>         <dbl>        <dbl>          <dbl>        <dbl>
1 GOBP_OX…           3.03          1.59         2.04           1.74         2.58
2 GOBP_RI…           2.64          1.81         1.81           1.61         2.03
3 GOBP_NU…           2.86          1.73         2.05           1.83         2.13
4 GOBP_NU…           2.39          1.94         1.74           1.60         2.06
5 GOBP_NU…           2.07          1.89         1.68           1.55         2.09
6 GOBP_MI…           1.91          1.60         1.56           1.65         1.92
# Reactome
common_upreg_reactome <- purrr::map(
  reactome_gsea.lst,
  ~ .x@result %>%
    dplyr::filter(NES > 0) %>%
    pull(ID)
) %>%
  Reduce(intersect, .)

common_reactome_nes.df <- purrr::map_dfr(
  names(reactome_gsea.lst),
  function(study_name){
    gsea <- reactome_gsea.lst[[study_name]]@result
    gsea_nes <- gsea %>%
      dplyr::filter(ID %in% common_upreg_reactome) %>%
      dplyr::select(ID, NES) %>%
      dplyr::mutate(study = study_name) 
    return(gsea_nes)
  }
)

# Change the data frame to wider form
common_reactome_nes.df <- common_reactome_nes.df  %>%
  tidyr::pivot_wider(
    names_from = study,
    values_from = NES
  )

head(common_reactome_nes.df)
# A tibble: 4 × 6
  ID       `Penter(2021)` `Nadeu(2022)` `Hing(2023)` `Parry(2023b)` `Rigo(2024)`
  <chr>             <dbl>         <dbl>        <dbl>          <dbl>        <dbl>
1 REACTOM…           3.35          2.35         2.24           1.82         2.35
2 REACTOM…           2.82          1.78         2.15           1.91         2.25
3 REACTOM…           2.44          2.33         1.70           1.89         1.78
4 REACTOM…           1.86          1.63         1.94           1.56         1.77
# Join the data frames using rbind()
common_enrichment_terms <- bind_rows(common_hm_nes.df, common_go_nes.df, common_reactome_nes.df)
common_enrichment_terms <- common_enrichment_terms %>%
  dplyr::rowwise() %>%
  dplyr::mutate(avrg_NES = mean(c_across(where(is.numeric)), na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  dplyr::ungroup()

head(common_enrichment_terms)
# A tibble: 6 × 7
  ID       `Penter(2021)` `Nadeu(2022)` `Hing(2023)` `Parry(2023b)` `Rigo(2024)`
  <chr>             <dbl>         <dbl>        <dbl>          <dbl>        <dbl>
1 HALLMAR…           3.15          2.11         2.04           1.36         2.95
2 HALLMAR…           3.01          2.06         2.06           2.09         1.44
3 HALLMAR…           2.24          2.24         2.11           2.01         1.61
4 HALLMAR…           2.22          1.89         1.89           1.82         1.68
5 HALLMAR…           1.77          1.42         1.77           2.07         1.84
6 HALLMAR…           1.86          2.15         1.65           1.70         2.39
# ℹ 1 more variable: avrg_NES <dbl>

Bar plot summarise enrichment terms

# Bar plot of GSEA 
selected_terms <- c("HALLMARK_OXIDATIVE_PHOSPHORYLATION", "HALLMARK_FATTY_ACID_METABOLISM", "HALLMARK_MYC_TARGETS_V1", "HALLMARK_E2F_TARGETS", "HALLMARK_MTORC1_SIGNALING",
                    "HALLMARK_G2M_CHECKPOINT", "REACTOME_METABOLISM_OF_NUCLEOTIDES", "GOBP_MITOCHONDRIAL_TRANSPORT", "REACTOME_MITOCHONDRIAL_TRANSLATION",
                    "GOBP_CHROMOSOME_SEGREGATION")
selected_enrichment.df <- common_enrichment_terms %>%
  dplyr::filter(ID %in% selected_terms) %>%
  dplyr::select(ID, avrg_NES) %>%
  dplyr::arrange(desc(avrg_NES))

selected_enrichment.df$ID <- gsub("HALLMARK_", "", selected_enrichment.df$ID)
selected_enrichment.df$ID <- gsub("GOBP_", "", selected_enrichment.df$ID)
selected_enrichment.df$ID <- gsub("REACTOME_", "", selected_enrichment.df$ID)
selected_enrichment.df$ID <- gsub("_V1", "", selected_enrichment.df$ID)
#selected_enrichment.df$ID <- gsub("OXIDATIVE_PHOSPHORYLATION", "OXPHOS", selected_enrichment.df$ID)
selected_enrichment.df$ID <- gsub("METABOLISM_OF_NUCLEOTIDES", "NUCLEOTIDE_METABOLISM", selected_enrichment.df$ID)
selected_enrichment.df$ID <- gsub("_", " ", selected_enrichment.df$ID)

selected_enrichment.df$ID <- rev(factor(selected_enrichment.df$ID, levels = selected_enrichment.df$ID))

gsea_bar.plt <- ggplot(selected_enrichment.df, aes(x=avrg_NES, y=ID)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = "#1f78b4", color="black", width = 0.75) +  
  theme_classic() +
  xlab("Average NES") + ylab(NULL) + 
  theme(legend.position = "none")

gsea_bar.plt

Correlation analysis for common enrichment terms

# Remove the gene column for correlation
gsea_mat <- common_enrichment_terms %>% dplyr::select(-c("ID", "avrg_NES")) %>% as.matrix()

# Compute correlations and cosine similarity
pearson_cor <- cor(gsea_mat, use = "pairwise.complete.obs", method = "pearson")
spearman_cor <- cor(gsea_mat, use = "pairwise.complete.obs", method = "spearman")
cosine_similar <- lsa::cosine(gsea_mat)

# Compute Euclidean similarity
euclidean_dist <- as.matrix(dist(t(gsea_mat), method = "euclidean"))
euclidean_similar <- 1 / (1 + euclidean_dist)
# Plot with viridis colour palette
plot_auto_contrast_heatmap(pearson_cor, "Pearson correlation (upregulated GSEA)")
plot_auto_contrast_heatmap(spearman_cor, "Spearman correlation (upregulated GSEA)")
plot_auto_contrast_heatmap(cosine_similar, "Cosine similarity (upregulated GSEA)")
plot_auto_contrast_heatmap(euclidean_similar, "Euclidean similarity (upregulated GSEA)")
